mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion that increases consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% for consecutive months, the zero lower bound constraint signals sustained monetary accommodation that suppresses discount rates and increases equity valuations, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal combining rising retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates accelerating consumer and residential demand that expands aggregate output and corporate profit margins, driving positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2%
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window, or joint trigger instances do not yield significantly higher 60-day returns than single-proxy triggers (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.3%, signaling supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction that reduces corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO months produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 during supply contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Rising unemployment periods produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.04 when UNRATE increases > 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL declines month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains accommodative (< 0.5%), signaling deflationary pressure that increases real discount rates and equity risk premiums, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: deflationary environment with monetary policy at zero lower bound
failure_condition: Joint deflationary-accommodation signal produces Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year window, or instances do not yield significantly lower 20-day returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter exceeds 2.5% annualized rate, signaling robust economic expansion that supports equity valuations and earnings growth, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13422.4 billion chained 2017 dollars in Q3 2011
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion above trend growth
failure_condition: High-growth quarters produce positive 60-day returns in < 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 when GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 600,000 units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month, indicating synchronized demand collapse that signals recession risk and equity market repricing, producing negative 5-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 600,000 units AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0%
regime_hint: demand-side recession or sharp cyclical downturn
failure_condition: Joint demand-collapse signal produces Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year window, or triggered instances do not yield significantly lower 5-day returns than non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.05).
